blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: If he helped someone for compensation, it's illegal. But what if Fermi just helped someone on a whim? Can we call that unfair?

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Can we call that unfair?
Olivia couldn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: The graduation exam candidates range from first to thirtieth place. But the gap between students isn't consistent every year. Some years, students of similar levels compete, while other years, a standout talent sweeps the competition. Fermi's case isn't different.
Olivia put a finger to her lips, pondering.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Hmm, if we're only talking about combat ability, he could probably compete at level eight. Honestly, below certified Class 6, it's all pretty similar. They just keep overtaking each other.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: We can say that from above, but for students, even certified Class 10 mages are a huge barrier. And that's normal. But there are always students who surpass the typical level. You can't argue fairness just because a few exceptional students are included.
Olivia groaned, resting her chin on her hand.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: There was no way to overthrow Fermi's regime. What was a mage with professional-level skills, suspected of unregistered practices, thinking by doing this?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: So there's no way to help Amy. It's unfortunate.
Olivia's eyes narrowed at Alpheas's leading question.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Are you sure Amy would have passed 100% without the collusion?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Based on talent alone, yes.
Olivia glared at Alpheas with cold eyes.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Karmis's daughter is renowned across the continent. She absorbs everything like a sponge. A jack-of-all-trades, you could say. And she's actually beautiful too.
Olivia sighed.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Being too pretty is a curse.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I've watched her since she was twelve. She'll get through this.
They both looked out the window.
The school, which had seen countless events over the year, was now as quiet as a stage after a performance.
Thus, the year at the magic school came to an end.
Heaven, Sixth Heaven, Zebul.
The city of angels, Zebul, has two main systems.
One is Inglis, where the Akashic Records are kept, and the other is the Great World War, Archangel Cariel's laboratory.
The Great World War, which had been devastated by Shirone's Photon Cannon, was now fully restored.
The central control system, a massive pillar, was the tallest structure in the Great World War.
Next to the pillar emitting multicolored light, a galactic telescope smoothly observed the movements of the stars.
The Great World War had regained its former appearance.
But Heaven had not.
Since Shirone's departure, a wind of change had blown through First Heaven, Shamain.
The citizens no longer believed in the absolute correctness of the Doctrine of Unity, and some radicals had emerged.
In response, the angels mobilized all their resources to break through Miro's Time and Space.
Leading the charge was Archangel Cariel.
The Archangel of Creation, who had tried to cast the Elixir of Life on Amy, Tess, and Arin, who had been captured by the fallen angel Ikasa.
The most intelligent of the angels, he analyzed the Meta Gate Shirone had left behind when he returned to his original world.
Finally, he found the coordinates to the Land of Earth.
But to get there, they had to break through Miro's Time and Space.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Even with the coordinates, we have to break through. But it's tricky.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Then what's the point of recovering the coordinates?
Cariel turned at the majestic voice.
Recognizing the voice, he smiled.
Another archangel, Uriel, clad in pure white heavy armor, floated in the air.
Angels are taller than humans, but Uriel was the tallest among them, standing at three meters.
His chest armor was so thick it reached his chin.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Not necessarily.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: There are quite a few Nephilim in the Land of Earth now.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Even if we break Miro's Time and Space, we can set up a firewall.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: But with the coordinates, we can bypass it.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Break Miro's Time and Space? Wasn't that strategy dismissed long ago?
Only Anke Ra could dismiss an archangel's proposal.
Indeed, Anke Ra had forbidden investigating the Land of Earth after the Final War was blocked by the Maze.
The archangels didn't know why, but they couldn't defy Anke Ra, who governed the Akashic Records.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: I don't intend to defy Ra's will.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It's a precaution.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: The Land of Earth will be conquered someday.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It might be too late to prepare then.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: So you're not certain about this experiment either.
Cariel smirked.
He had never been uncertain before.
But every attempt had been thwarted by Miro's quick response.
It felt like he was playing a game with her from an unseen place.
But today, she would have to give up.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Miro's Time and Space would be breached.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Anke Ra wouldn't want it, but... what could he do?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It was fate.
Cariel flew past the galactic telescope to the western wall.
Uriel watched for a moment before creating a terrifying gust of wind and catching up in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Here it is, my masterpiece for today.
Uriel couldn't find words to describe it.
Cariel's masterpiece was so massive it occupied a quarter of the Great World War.
A rectangular wall, 40 meters wide and 70 meters high.
Uriel, who had seemed like a giant earlier, now felt small in front of the wall.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: It's quite large.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Uriel's acknowledgment made Cariel even more proud.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: I wondered why we couldn't break through Miro's Time and Space.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It's because the dimensional wall is inherently unbreakable.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: But with this Celestial Wall, it's possible.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Even unbreakable liquid shatters when frozen.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: We'll strike Miro's Time and Space directly.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: A direct hit.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: So this Celestial Wall represents Miro's Time and Space?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It was a simplistic but accurate interpretation.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Well, it didn't matter.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: He had called Uriel for his strength, not his intellect.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: If the Celestial Wall breaks, so will Miro's Time and Space.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: I wanted to add an amplification circuit, but it's impossible without Ikael's help.
Uriel avoided responding.
Ikael wouldn't help.
She was imprisoned for an unforgivable crime, and Shirone's actions had solidified suspicions that she sided with humans.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: So, want to give it a try?

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: If the Celestial Wall breaks, so will Miro's Time and Space.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: And that went against Anke Ra's will.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: No one can defy Ra's will.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Not because he's strong, but because he's the whole.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Whatever the future held, Ra would remain Ra.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Cariel knew this, which was why he was taking such a reckless action.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Why... did Ikael abandon us?
Angels had long conveyed and executed God's will in the Land of Earth.
Uriel, in particular, had excelled in destruction.
His power overturned the earth and split the sea.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: But even Uriel, who had blindly slaughtered humans, questioned his righteousness when he heard rumors that Ikael might side with humans.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ikael was always right.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: No, it wasn't about right or wrong.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Even if she wasn't right, he wanted to follow her.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: If it was his nature to do so, he would dedicate himself to it, even if it was wrong.
Cariel probably felt the same way throughout his existence.
Angels had no concept of a mother.
But if they had to choose one, it would be Ikael.
The archangel who had cared for them since their birth.
Uriel glanced at Cariel.
Seeing his innocent smile reminded him of long ago.
Cariel hadn't always been like this.
He was still sly and perverted, but he hadn't been obsessed with creating things.
He used to make fun and ingenious items to give to Ikael, who would laugh and pat him.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Cariel, I can destroy it.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: You know that.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Do you really want this?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Haha!

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: This time won't be easy.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: No, honestly, it has to be destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: I want to annihilate the Land of Earth as soon as possible.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: If we break Miro's Time and Space, today will be that day.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Why are you in such a hurry?

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ra hasn't given any orders.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ymir is still asleep.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Is this really necessary?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Shirone has changed Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: And this is just the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: We must break through the Miro's Time and Space and eliminate the humans on Earth as soon as possible.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Cariel's concern was understandable.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: But it didn't make sense for an archangel who had destroyed countless earthly nations to be so anxious about a mere rebellion.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Do you think destroying humans will bring Ikael back?
Cariel's face suddenly hardened.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: ...What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Ikael's crime has already been erased.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: The incident itself no longer exists.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Goffin disappeared as a result.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Shouldn't we at least forgive her?
Cariel's halo expanded at a terrifying speed.
As the 40-meter-wide halo spread, the Great World War's mecha system sounded an emergency alarm, reflecting Cariel's current state of mind.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Erased?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Then what are these filthy memories left with us?

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: I don't care about Goffin!

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: It's Ikael who disgusts me!

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: Don't ever mention her again!

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Are you really going to fight her?

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Cariel, I'm asking again.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: Are you really going to fight her?
Cariel's expression was a mix of sorrow and anger.

blocks.speaker: Cariel
blocks.content: She gave birth to a human child!
The halo sent out wave-like ripples.
As the mecha system flickered on and off, the Great World War blinked.
